questions.name: Required Field Completeness
questions.evidence: model_details.name='ClimateNet-v2'; overview ~600 chars describing CNN for extreme weather; licenses=[{identifier:'BSD-3-Clause'}]; version.name='v2.0.0'; model_parameters.model_architecture describes ResNet-50 FPN with dual-head, 38.5M params
questions.quality_note: All five required fields populated with concrete, non-trivial content

questions.name: Overview Length Adequacy
questions.evidence: overview is ~600 chars covering purpose, inputs, outputs, and DOE mission; documentation block adds architecture sections, training details, citation pointers; short_description present too
questions.quality_note: Overview + documentation comfortably exceed 500 chars and are well-structured

questions.name: Tag / Keyword Diversity
questions.score_label: >=4 tags AND pipeline_tag but no language/model_category
questions.evidence: tags=[climate, extreme-weather, segmentation, object-detection, scientific-ml, doe, nersc] (7 tags); pipeline_tag='image-segmentation'; no top-level language or model_category field
questions.quality_note: Strong tag set and pipeline_tag, but neither language nor model_category present so full 5 not awarded

questions.name: Input / Output Specification
questions.evidence: input_format: shape (768,768,16), 16 channels enumerated (T,U,V,Q,Z,PSL,TMQ,PRECT), Z-score normalization, HDF5 format; output_format: segmentation mask (768,768) uint8 with class labels + bbox tuples + HDF5/GeoTIFF; no input_format_map or output_format_map populated
questions.quality_note: Inputs and outputs specified with shapes and types but the structured *_format_map fields are not provided

questions.name: Schema Version Declared
questions.evidence: schema_version: '2.0.0-extended'
questions.quality_note: Recognizable schema version string at top level

questions.name: Persistent Identifier Present
questions.evidence: references include 'https://github.com/lbnl-climate/climatenet-v2' (GitHub repo) and 'https://climatenet.lbl.gov' project site; installation also points to GHCR container 'ghcr.io/lbnl-climate/climatenet-v2:latest' and weights URL 'https://climatenet.lbl.gov/models/climatenet-v2.0.0.pth'
questions.quality_note: Multiple resolvable URIs that act as model identifiers; no DOI but GitHub release / project landing satisfy criterion

questions.name: Funding & Acknowledgements Completeness
questions.evidence: mission_relevance.funding_source: 'DOE Office of Science, Biological and Environmental Research (BER), Grant: DE-AC02-05CH11231'; doe_facility: 'NERSC Perlmutter'; contributor entry 'DOE Office of Science' with role funded_by
questions.quality_note: Agency, specific grant number, and computing facility all explicitly named

questions.name: Ethical & Responsible-AI Documentation
questions.score_label: Ethical considerations + out_of_scope_uses, no explicit bias_* fields
questions.evidence: considerations.ethical_considerations has two named items ('Climate justice implications', 'Model uncertainty communication') each with mitigation_strategy; out_of_scope_uses lists three concrete uses to avoid; however bias_model / bias_output / bias_input fields are not populated
questions.quality_note: Strong ethics + out-of-scope content but no dedicated bias disclosure field, so falls short of the 5 criterion

questions.name: License Clarity & SPDX Compliance
questions.score_label: SPDX identifier present, no explicit restrictions
questions.evidence: model_details.licenses[0].identifier='BSD-3-Clause' with license_name and OSI link; no custom_text describing restrictions
questions.quality_note: Clean SPDX identifier but explicit restriction/usage text not stated

questions.name: Framework / Library Standardization
questions.score_label: Framework + version pinned + library_name, no model_index
questions.evidence: framework='PyTorch', framework_version='2.1.0', library_name='climatenet'; no Papers-with-Code-shaped model_index block present
questions.quality_note: Framework standardization strong but model_index field missing for full 5

questions.name: Tool & Software Transparency
questions.evidence: compute_infrastructure.software lists PyTorch 2.1.0+CUDA 12.1, Horovod 0.28.1, NVIDIA Apex, Python 3.10; software_dependencies pins exact conda versions for h5py, xarray, dask, pandas, numpy, scikit-learn; usage_documentation references Docker image 'ghcr.io/lbnl-climate/climatenet-v2:latest' and environment.yml
questions.quality_note: Comprehensive: pinned framework, full env list, Docker image, and conda recipe

questions.name: Training Procedure Clarity
questions.score_label: Optimizer + LR + batch + epochs + schedule + regularization
questions.evidence: hyperparameters: optimizer='AdamW', learning_rate=0.0001, batch_size=2 per GPU (effective 512), training_epochs=40, training_steps=24000; optimization_techniques include mixed precision, gradient clipping max_norm=1.0, cosine annealing schedule (T_max=40), 2-epoch warmup; regularization: weight_decay 1e-4, dropout 0.1, label smoothing 0.1
questions.quality_note: All elements of the rubric criterion explicitly disclosed

questions.name: Version History Documentation
questions.evidence: version.name='v2.0.0' (semver); version.date='2024-11-15' (ISO); diff enumerates four substantive changes (ResNet-34 to ResNet-50, atmospheric-river capability, +15% TC boundary accuracy, 3x more training data)
questions.quality_note: All three required version elements meaningfully populated

questions.name: Citations & References
questions.score_label: One citation + multiple references with URLs (no DOIs)
questions.evidence: citations has one IEEE-style citation to SC24 proceedings (no BibTeX, no DOI); references lists 2 URLs (GitHub repo, project site); baselines text mentions Prabhat et al. 2021 inline
questions.quality_note: Solid but only a single formal citation and no DOIs, so not full 5

questions.name: Compute Infrastructure & Energy
questions.score_label: Hardware + software + total compute, no carbon/energy estimate
questions.evidence: hardware: 64 nodes x 4 A100 40GB (256 GPUs total), AMD EPYC 7763, Slingshot-11; software stack pinned; training_speed reports 32,768 GPU-hours total and per-epoch throughput; no kWh or CO2 estimate
questions.quality_note: GPU-hours quantified but no explicit energy or carbon footprint reported

questions.name: Findability (Persistent Landing)
questions.evidence: references include GitHub repo and project landing page; usage docs provide GHCR container URL and direct weights download URL
questions.quality_note: Multiple resolvable landing URLs documented

questions.name: Accessibility & Inference Path
questions.score_label: Runnable code + I/O formats + library hooks
questions.evidence: Three runnable code_examples: Python inference (ClimateNetV2.from_pretrained, model(patch), outputs['segmentation']), Python evaluation (compute_metrics), bash SLURM batch script; installation_instructions cover conda, Docker, NERSC module; input_format and output_format documented
questions.quality_note: Full reproducible inference path with multiple entry points

questions.name: Performance Metrics with Slices & CI
questions.evidence: performance_metrics has 5 entries with numeric values: IoU TC 0.847 [0.841,0.853] slice 'Test set (E3SM v2)'; IoU AR 0.782 [0.774,0.790] same slice; IoU Fronts 0.691 [0.681,0.701]; mAP@0.5 0.823 slice 'Test set (all events)'; throughput 125.3. Multiple slices and bootstrap CIs on three metrics; ensemble sigma=0.012 reported
questions.quality_note: Meets and exceeds threshold: multiple metrics, multiple slices, explicit confidence intervals

questions.name: Out-of-Scope Uses, Limitations & Tradeoffs
questions.score_label: All three populated with concrete, model-specific content
questions.evidence: limitations: 3 entries (coarse-resolution training, atmospheric-only scope, polar accuracy degradation >70 deg); tradeoffs: 2 entries (resolution vs throughput; backbone vs IoU); out_of_scope_uses: 3 entries (real-time forecasting, satellite imagery, attribution)
questions.quality_note: All three fields contain specific, non-boilerplate, model-relevant statements

questions.name: Cross-Platform Interlinks
questions.evidence: model_parameters.data links three external dataset projects (CAM5.1, E3SM v2, MPAS-A) with URLs; references link to GitHub repo and project site; citations point to SC24 proceedings
questions.quality_note: Cross-platform links to external dataset records and external publication present

strengths: Comprehensive training-procedure disclosure: optimizer, schedule, regularization, distributed-training topology, and seed all explicit
Strong reproducibility surface: pinned framework/library versions, conda environment, Docker image, and SLURM launch script
Rich performance reporting with multiple metrics, slices, and bootstrap confidence intervals
All three responsible-AI considerations populated (limitations, tradeoffs, out_of_scope_uses) with model-specific content
Clear funding attribution with DOE office, BER program, grant number, and NERSC facility
weaknesses: No bias_model / bias_output / bias_input fields populated — bias disclosure happens only obliquely through ethical_considerations
Compute reporting lacks an energy or carbon estimate despite GPU-hour totals being available
model_index (Papers-with-Code shape) is not provided, limiting machine-readable benchmark integration
License lacks explicit restriction text and citations have no DOIs or BibTeX entries
Input/output specifications are prose-only; *_format_map structured fields are unused
recommendations: Add explicit bias_model / bias_output / bias_input entries describing geographic, seasonal, and resolution-related biases
Estimate energy consumption (kWh) and CO2-equivalent from the reported 32,768 GPU-hours on NERSC Perlmutter
Populate model_index in Papers-with-Code form with task, dataset, and metric triples to enable cross-platform benchmark discovery
Add a DOI for the SC24 paper and include a BibTeX block in citations
Populate input_format_map and output_format_map with structured per-channel / per-class metadata
